query: What are the requirements and fees required to submit a valid interconnection application for a large generator in ISO New England, based on the latest ISO-NE Interconnection Process Guide and ISO-NE Tariff Schedule 22 Large Generator Interconnection Procedures (LGIP)?
final_answer:
Below is a practical checklist for a **valid ISO New England large-generator interconnection application / Interconnection Request (IR)** under the current **ISO-NE Interconnection Process Guide** and **ISO-NE OATT Schedule 22 LGIP**.

**Scope assumption:** I treat “large generator” as a **Large Generating Facility**, i.e., a generating facility with **maximum gross capability at or above 0°F of more than 20 MW**, as defined in ISO-NE Tariff Schedule 22 LGIP. Source: ISO-NE OATT Schedule 22 LGIP, Definitions, “Large Generating Facility,” effective 5/3/25, https://www.iso-ne.com/static-assets/documents/regulatory/tariff/sect_2/sch22/sch_22_lgip.pdf.

## 1) When and how to submit

A valid IR must be submitted **through ISO-NE’s Interconnection Request Tracking Tool (IRTT)** and only **during a 45-calendar-day Cluster Request Window**; requests submitted outside the window “will not be considered.” Sources: ISO-NE Interconnection Process Guide, https://www.iso-ne.com/participate/applications-status-changes/interconnection-process-guide; ISO-NE “Submit an Interconnection Request During Cluster Request Window,” https://www.iso-ne.com/participate/applications-status-changes/interconnection-process-guide/submit-an-interconnection-request; ISO-NE Schedule 22 LGIP §§3.4.1 and 3.4.2.

The ISO-NE guide states that, before submission, a customer should have confirmed eligibility, received an **ISO customer ID**, obtained **Interconnection Request CEII clearance**, and created an **IRTT account**. Source: ISO-NE “Submit an Interconnection Request During Cluster Request Window,” https://www.iso-ne.com/participate/applications-status-changes/interconnection-process-guide/submit-an-interconnection-request.

As of the current ISO-NE Interconnection Process Guide, the next Cluster Study request window is expected to run **October 5, 2026 to November 19, 2026**. Source: ISO-NE Interconnection Process Guide, https://www.iso-ne.com/participate/applications-status-changes/interconnection-process-guide.

## 2) Core application contents required for validity

To initiate and establish a valid Large Generator IR, the Interconnection Customer must submit all of the following to ISO-NE in the manner specified in **Schedule 22 Appendix 1**. Source: ISO-NE Schedule 22 LGIP §3.4.2 and Appendix 1, https://www.iso-ne.com/static-assets/documents/regulatory/tariff/sect_2/sch22/sch_22_lgip.pdf; Appendix 1 form, https://www.iso-ne.com/static-assets/documents/100030/oatt-schedule-22-appendix-1-large-generator-lgip-interconnection-request-with-attachments.pdf.

| Requirement | What must be provided | Source |
|---|---:|---|
| Completed application | Completed **Appendix 1 Interconnection Request** and all information required by its attachments. | ISO-NE Schedule 22 LGIP §3.4.2(ii); Appendix 1 form. |
| Technical data | All technical data required in the Appendix 1 attachments must be submitted in IRTT with the IR. | ISO-NE Schedule 22 Appendix 1, Attachment A, “Technical Data Required For Cluster Study.” |
| Project type | Identify whether the request is for a new Large Generating Facility, an increase/modification that may be a Material Modification, market participation by an existing generating facility, or a change from NR to CNR service. | ISO-NE Schedule 22 Appendix 1 form. |
| Interconnection service type | Select **NR Interconnection Service** or **CNR Interconnection Service** at the time of IR submission. NR is energy capability only; CNR is energy and capacity capability. | ISO-NE Schedule 22 LGIP §§3.2 and 3.4.2(ix); ISO-NE “Submit an Interconnection Request” guide. |
| Facility capability and requested service level | Provide Generating Facility capability in MW and the requested Interconnection Service level if it is below full facility capability. | ISO-NE Schedule 22 LGIP §3.4.2(vi). |
| Point of Interconnection | Provide a Point of Interconnection. | ISO-NE Schedule 22 LGIP §3.4.2(viii). |
| Site/location map | Include a detailed map, such as a U.S. Geological Survey-quality map, clearly showing the facility site and pertinent surrounding structures. | ISO-NE Schedule 22 Appendix 1 form. |
| Site Control | Provide required Site Control evidence, or for qualifying NR requests only, the permitted Regulatory Limitations package plus deposit in lieu of Site Control. | ISO-NE Schedule 22 LGIP §3.4.2(iv)–(v); ISO-NE Site Control Technical Notice, https://www.iso-ne.com/static-assets/documents/100024/notice_re_regulatory_limitations_and_site_control_6_2_25.pdf. |
| Shared-site documentation | If multiple Interconnection Customers submit IRs for multiple Generating Facilities sharing a site, include a contract or other agreement allowing shared land use. | ISO-NE Schedule 22 LGIP §3.4.2(v); Schedule 22 Appendix 1 form. |
| Control technology, if applicable | If requesting Interconnection Service below full Large Generating Facility capability, include proposed control technologies to restrict output to the requested service level. | ISO-NE Schedule 22 LGIP §3.1. |
| Deposits | Submit the initial deposit, study deposit, Commercial Readiness Deposit, and any site-control-in-lieu deposit if applicable. | ISO-NE Schedule 22 LGIP §§3.4.1.1 and 3.4.2. |

## 3) Site Control requirement

**Site Control** means the exclusive right to develop, construct, operate, and maintain the Generating Facility over its expected operating term, and it must be of sufficient size to construct and operate the facility. Source: ISO-NE Schedule 22 LGIP, Definitions, “Site Control.”

Site Control may be shown by documentation establishing one of the following: fee ownership or easement; valid written leasehold or other contractual interest; valid written option to purchase or lease; executed contract to purchase, acquire an easement, license, or leasehold interest; or filed applications for required permits to site on federal or state property. Sources: ISO-NE Schedule 22 LGIP, Definitions, “Site Control”; ISO-NE Site Control Technical Notice.

For **CNR Interconnection Service**, the customer must demonstrate **100% Site Control**. Sources: ISO-NE Schedule 22 LGIP §3.4.2(iv); ISO-NE Site Control Technical Notice.

For **NR Interconnection Service**, the customer must demonstrate **100% Site Control**, unless a qualifying **Regulatory Limitation** prevents that demonstration. In that case, the customer must submit an officer affidavit/attestation, documentation describing the source and effects of the Regulatory Limitation and expected path/timing to satisfy it, and a cash deposit in lieu of Site Control. Sources: ISO-NE Schedule 22 LGIP §3.4.2(v); ISO-NE Site Control Technical Notice.

A Regulatory Limitation is a federal, state, Tribal, or local law that makes it practically infeasible to obtain Site Control within the Schedule 22 timeframe; ISO-NE’s notice states that ordinary permitting or siting issues are not intended to qualify as Regulatory Limitations. Sources: ISO-NE Schedule 22 LGIP, Definitions, “Regulatory Limitations”; ISO-NE Site Control Technical Notice.

## 4) Fees and deposits due with the IR

For a typical **new Large Generator IR** seeking either **NR** or **CNR** service and providing Site Control, the required submission package includes **$800,000** in initial/study/readiness deposits: **$50,000 initial deposit + $250,000 study deposit + $500,000 Commercial Readiness Deposit**. Sources: ISO-NE Schedule 22 LGIP §§3.4.1.1 and 3.4.2(vii).

| Deposit / fee | Amount | Applicability | Refundability / use | Source |
|---|---:|---|---|---|
| Initial deposit | **$50,000** | All Large Generator IRs submitted during the Cluster Request Window. | Potentially non-refundable; applied to ISO-NE costs associated with the IR, study agreements/attachments, and LGIA development. | ISO-NE Schedule 22 LGIP §§3.4.1.1 and 3.4.2(i). |
| Cluster Study deposit — new NR or CNR request | **$250,000** | New Large Generator requests for NR or CNR Interconnection Service. | Refundable study deposit; applied toward Cluster Study cost. | ISO-NE Schedule 22 LGIP §3.4.1.1. |
| Cluster Study deposit — CNR-only / NR-to-CNR category | **$100,000** | IRs for which NR Interconnection Service studies have been completed but CNR has not been achieved, or requests to change existing NR Interconnection Service to CNR Interconnection Service. | Refundable study deposit; applied toward Cluster Study cost. | ISO-NE Schedule 22 LGIP §3.4.1.1. |
| Commercial Readiness Deposit for new NR/CNR request | **2× study deposit = $500,000** | New Large Generator NR or CNR request using the $250,000 study deposit. | Refunded according to Schedule 22 §3.7; may be cash, irrevocable letter of credit, surety bond, or combination. | ISO-NE Schedule 22 LGIP §§3.4.2(vii) and 3.1. |
| Commercial Readiness Deposit for $100,000-study-deposit category | **2× study deposit = $200,000** | CNR-only / NR-to-CNR category using the $100,000 study deposit. | Refunded according to Schedule 22 §3.7; may be cash, irrevocable letter of credit, surety bond, or combination. | ISO-NE Schedule 22 LGIP §§3.4.1.1, 3.4.2(vii), and 3.1. |
| Site Control deposit in lieu — NR only with qualifying Regulatory Limitation | **$10,000/MW**, minimum **$500,000**, maximum **$2,000,000** | Only for NR Interconnection Service where qualifying Regulatory Limitations prevent 100% Site Control at submission. | Cash deposit held until required Site Control demonstration is provided; not available for CNR requests. | ISO-NE Schedule 22 LGIP §3.4.2(v); ISO-NE Site Control Technical Notice. |

**Form of payment/security:** Commercial Readiness Deposits may be provided by electronic cash transfer, acceptable irrevocable letter of credit, acceptable surety bond, or a combination; all other ISO-NE deposits under the LGIP must be paid in cash by electronic transfer to ISO-NE’s bank account unless otherwise stated. Source: ISO-NE Schedule 22 LGIP §3.1.

A deposit is not considered received until it is in ISO-NE’s bank account, or, for a Commercial Readiness Deposit provided as a letter of credit or surety bond, until the letter of credit or surety bond is accepted by ISO-NE. Source: ISO-NE Schedule 22 LGIP §3.1.

## 5) Minimum dollar examples

| Scenario | Required amount at submission, excluding optional/conditional items | Additional conditional amount | Source |
|---|---:|---:|---|
| New Large Generator, NR or CNR, with 100% Site Control | **$800,000** = $50,000 initial + $250,000 study + $500,000 CRD | None, if Site Control is accepted. | ISO-NE Schedule 22 LGIP §§3.4.1.1 and 3.4.2. |
| New Large Generator, NR only, qualifying Regulatory Limitation instead of Site Control | **$800,000** base deposits | Plus **$10,000/MW**, min **$500,000**, max **$2,000,000**, cash site-control-in-lieu deposit. | ISO-NE Schedule 22 LGIP §3.4.2(v); ISO-NE Site Control Technical Notice. |
| CNR-only / existing NR-to-CNR category | **$350,000** = $50,000 initial + $100,000 study + $200,000 CRD | Site Control still required for CNR. | ISO-NE Schedule 22 LGIP §§3.4.1.1 and 3.4.2. |

## 6) Deficiency review and cure

An IR is **not valid** until all items required by Schedule 22 §3.4.2 have been received by ISO-NE during the Cluster Request Window. Source: ISO-NE Schedule 22 LGIP §3.4.4.

If ISO-NE finds the IR deficient, it must notify the customer within **5 Business Days** of

steps.reasoning:
**Researching interconnection requirements**

I’m trying to find the latest ISO-NE Interconnection Process Guide and Tariff Schedule 22 LGIP. It’s important to find out the requirements and fees for submitting a valid interconnection application for large generators. I’ll need to make sure to cite the information properly too. Alright, let’s go ahead and search the web for this to gather what I need!

tool_input.query: ISO New England Interconnection Process Guide latest Large Generator Interconnection Procedures Schedule 22 valid Interconnection Request deposit fee Site Control requirements

steps.reasoning:
**Searching for interconnection answers**

I need to find the requirements and fees for a valid interconnection application for a large generator based on the latest guide and tariff. To make sure I get accurate information, I should look up the relevant guide and search for the necessary details. It seems like a good approach to confirm I've covered everything. Let's see what I can find!

tool_input.query: site:iso-ne.com Interconnection Process Guide ISO New England PDF 2025 valid interconnection request large generator cluster study deposit commercial readiness deposit

steps.reasoning:
**Searching for application details**

I need to find the requirements and fees for submitting a valid application for a large generator based on the latest guide and Schedule 22 LGIP. It seems like I'm specifically looking for details in Section 3.4, but my search result is only partial. I'll have to look for the full PDF to extract the complete contents for more clarity. highlights.query: Valid Interconnection Request Cluster Study Deposit Commercial Readiness Deposit Site Control application requirements fees large generator

steps.reasoning:
**Gathering interconnection application details**

I need to find the exact requirements and fees for submitting a valid interconnection application for a large generator, referring to the latest guide and the LGIP. I should gather the specific text from section 3.4 of the LGIP, including the CRD amounts. Searching the PDF highlights might not be enough, so I might consider using Python to download the PDF and extract the text with pdftotext. Let’s fetch that information!

tool_input.code:
import requests, re
url='https://www.iso-ne.com/static-assets/documents/regulatory/tariff/sect_2/sch22/sch_22_lgip.pdf'
r=requests.get(url,timeout=20)
open('/tmp/lgip.pdf','wb').write(r.content)
print(len(r.content), r.content[:4])

tool_input.command:
python - <<'PY'
import PyPDF2, re
path='/tmp/lgip.pdf'
reader=PyPDF2.PdfReader(path)
text='\n'.join(page.extract_text() or '' for page in reader.pages)
print('pages',len(reader.pages),'chars',len(text))
for term in ['3.4 Valid Interconnection Request','3.4.2','Commercial Readiness Deposit','initial deposit','Cluster Study Deposit','Valid Interconnection Request','Appendix 1']:
 print('\nTERM',term)
 i=text.find(term)
 print(i)
 print(text[i-1000:i+4000] if i!=-1 else '')
PY
pdftotext /tmp/lgip.pdf - | grep -n -C 4 "3.4.2\|Commercial Readiness Deposit\|Cluster Study Deposit\|initial deposit\|Valid Interconnection Request" | head -n 300

curl -L -o lgip.pdf 'https://www.iso-ne.com/static-assets/documents/regulatory/tariff/sect_2/sch22/sch_22_lgip.pdf' >/dev/null 2>&1 && pdftotext lgip.pdf - | grep -n -C 4 "3.4.2\|Commercial Readiness Deposit\|Cluster Study Deposit\|initial deposit\|Valid Interconnection Request" | head -n 400

pdftotext lgip.pdf lgip.txt && sed -n '1218,1345p' lgip.txt && echo '---' && sed -n '1364,1411p' lgip.txt && echo '---appendix' && sed -n '4370,4425p' lgip.txt && echo '--- forms' && sed -n '955,996p' lgip.txt

grep -n "Large Generating Facility shall mean" -A5 lgip.txt | head -20 && grep -n "Generating Facility capability" -A5 lgip.txt | head -20 && grep -n "Site Control shall mean" -A20 lgip.txt | head -60
grep -n "3.1" -A80 lgip.txt | head -120 && grep -n "Types of Interconnection Service" -A120 lgip.txt | head -150

steps.content:
Below is a practical checklist for a **valid ISO New England large-generator interconnection application / Interconnection Request (IR)** under the current **ISO-NE Interconnection Process Guide** and **ISO-NE OATT Schedule 22 LGIP**.

**Scope assumption:** I treat “large generator” as a **Large Generating Facility**, i.e., a generating facility with **maximum gross capability at or above 0°F of more than 20 MW**, as defined in ISO-NE Tariff Schedule 22 LGIP. Source: ISO-NE OATT Schedule 22 LGIP, Definitions, “Large Generating Facility,” effective 5/3/25, https://www.iso-ne.com/static-assets/documents/regulatory/tariff/sect_2/sch22/sch_22_lgip.pdf.

## 1) When and how to submit

A valid IR must be submitted **through ISO-NE’s Interconnection Request Tracking Tool (IRTT)** and only **during a 45-calendar-day Cluster Request Window**; requests submitted outside the window “will not be considered.” Sources: ISO-NE Interconnection Process Guide, https://www.iso-ne.com/participate/applications-status-changes/interconnection-process-guide; ISO-NE “Submit an Interconnection Request During Cluster Request Window,” https://www.iso-ne.com/participate/applications-status-changes/interconnection-process-guide/submit-an-interconnection-request; ISO-NE Schedule 22 LGIP §§3.4.1 and 3.4.2.

The ISO-NE guide states that, before submission, a customer should have confirmed eligibility, received an **ISO customer ID**, obtained **Interconnection Request CEII clearance**, and created an **IRTT account**. Source: ISO-NE “Submit an Interconnection Request During Cluster Request Window,” https://www.iso-ne.com/participate/applications-status-changes/interconnection-process-guide/submit-an-interconnection-request.

As of the current ISO-NE Interconnection Process Guide, the next Cluster Study request window is expected to run **October 5, 2026 to November 19, 2026**. Source: ISO-NE Interconnection Process Guide, https://www.iso-ne.com/participate/applications-status-changes/interconnection-process-guide.

## 2) Core application contents required for validity

To initiate and establish a valid Large Generator IR, the Interconnection Customer must submit all of the following to ISO-NE in the manner specified in **Schedule 22 Appendix 1**. Source: ISO-NE Schedule 22 LGIP §3.4.2 and Appendix 1, https://www.iso-ne.com/static-assets/documents/regulatory/tariff/sect_2/sch22/sch_22_lgip.pdf; Appendix 1 form, https://www.iso-ne.com/static-assets/documents/100030/oatt-schedule-22-appendix-1-large-generator-lgip-interconnection-request-with-attachments.pdf.

| Requirement | What must be provided | Source |
|---|---:|---|
| Completed application | Completed **Appendix 1 Interconnection Request** and all information required by its attachments. | ISO-NE Schedule 22 LGIP §3.4.2(ii); Appendix 1 form. |
| Technical data | All technical data required in the Appendix 1 attachments must be submitted in IRTT with the IR. | ISO-NE Schedule 22 Appendix 1, Attachment A, “Technical Data Required For Cluster Study.” |
| Project type | Identify whether the request is for a new Large Generating Facility, an increase/modification that may be a Material Modification, market participation by an existing generating facility, or a change from NR to CNR service. | ISO-NE Schedule 22 Appendix 1 form. |
| Interconnection service type | Select **NR Interconnection Service** or **CNR Interconnection Service** at the time of IR submission. NR is energy capability only; CNR is energy and capacity capability. | ISO-NE Schedule 22 LGIP §§3.2 and 3.4.2(ix); ISO-NE “Submit an Interconnection Request” guide. |
| Facility capability and requested service level | Provide Generating Facility capability in MW and the requested Interconnection Service level if it is below full facility capability. | ISO-NE Schedule 22 LGIP §3.4.2(vi). |
| Point of Interconnection | Provide a Point of Interconnection. | ISO-NE Schedule 22 LGIP §3.4.2(viii). |
| Site/location map | Include a detailed map, such as a U.S. Geological Survey-quality map, clearly showing the facility site and pertinent surrounding structures. | ISO-NE Schedule 22 Appendix 1 form. |
| Site Control | Provide required Site Control evidence, or for qualifying NR requests only, the permitted Regulatory Limitations package plus deposit in lieu of Site Control. | ISO-NE Schedule 22 LGIP §3.4.2(iv)–(v); ISO-NE Site Control Technical Notice, https://www.iso-ne.com/static-assets/documents/100024/notice_re_regulatory_limitations_and_site_control_6_2_25.pdf. |
| Shared-site documentation | If multiple Interconnection Customers submit IRs for multiple Generating Facilities sharing a site, include a contract or other agreement allowing shared land use. | ISO-NE Schedule 22 LGIP §3.4.2(v); Schedule 22 Appendix 1 form. |
| Control technology, if applicable | If requesting Interconnection Service below full Large Generating Facility capability, include proposed control technologies to restrict output to the requested service level. | ISO-NE Schedule 22 LGIP §3.1. |
| Deposits | Submit the initial deposit, study deposit, Commercial Readiness Deposit, and any site-control-in-lieu deposit if applicable. | ISO-NE Schedule 22 LGIP §§3.4.1.1 and 3.4.2. |

## 3) Site Control requirement

**Site Control** means the exclusive right to develop, construct, operate, and maintain the Generating Facility over its expected operating term, and it must be of sufficient size to construct and operate the facility. Source: ISO-NE Schedule 22 LGIP, Definitions, “Site Control.”

Site Control may be shown by documentation establishing one of the following: fee ownership or easement; valid written leasehold or other contractual interest; valid written option to purchase or lease; executed contract to purchase, acquire an easement, license, or leasehold interest; or filed applications for required permits to site on federal or state property. Sources: ISO-NE Schedule 22 LGIP, Definitions, “Site Control”; ISO-NE Site Control Technical Notice.

For **CNR Interconnection Service**, the customer must demonstrate **100% Site Control**. Sources: ISO-NE Schedule 22 LGIP §3.4.2(iv); ISO-NE Site Control Technical Notice.

For **NR Interconnection Service**, the customer must demonstrate **100% Site Control**, unless a qualifying **Regulatory Limitation** prevents that demonstration. In that case, the customer must submit an officer affidavit/attestation, documentation describing the source and effects of the Regulatory Limitation and expected path/timing to satisfy it, and a cash deposit in lieu of Site Control. Sources: ISO-NE Schedule 22 LGIP §3.4.2(v); ISO-NE Site Control Technical Notice.

A Regulatory Limitation is a federal, state, Tribal, or local law that makes it practically infeasible to obtain Site Control within the Schedule 22 timeframe; ISO-NE’s notice states that ordinary permitting or siting issues are not intended to qualify as Regulatory Limitations. Sources: ISO-NE Schedule 22 LGIP, Definitions, “Regulatory Limitations”; ISO-NE Site Control Technical Notice.

## 4) Fees and deposits due with the IR

For a typical **new Large Generator IR** seeking either **NR** or **CNR** service and providing Site Control, the required submission package includes **$800,000** in initial/study/readiness deposits: **$50,000 initial deposit + $250,000 study deposit + $500,000 Commercial Readiness Deposit**. Sources: ISO-NE Schedule 22 LGIP §§3.4.1.1 and 3.4.2(vii).

| Deposit / fee | Amount | Applicability | Refundability / use | Source |
|---|---:|---|---|---|
| Initial deposit | **$50,000** | All Large Generator IRs submitted during the Cluster Request Window. | Potentially non-refundable; applied to ISO-NE costs associated with the IR, study agreements/attachments, and LGIA development. | ISO-NE Schedule 22 LGIP §§3.4.1.1 and 3.4.2(i). |
| Cluster Study deposit — new NR or CNR request | **$250,000** | New Large Generator requests for NR or CNR Interconnection Service. | Refundable study deposit; applied toward Cluster Study cost. | ISO-NE Schedule 22 LGIP §3.4.1.1. |
| Cluster Study deposit — CNR-only / NR-to-CNR category | **$100,000** | IRs for which NR Interconnection Service studies have been completed but CNR has not been achieved, or requests to change existing NR Interconnection Service to CNR Interconnection Service. | Refundable study deposit; applied toward Cluster Study cost. | ISO-NE Schedule 22 LGIP §3.4.1.1. |
| Commercial Readiness Deposit for new NR/CNR request | **2× study deposit = $500,000** | New Large Generator NR or CNR request using the $250,000 study deposit. | Refunded according to Schedule 22 §3.7; may be cash, irrevocable letter of credit, surety bond, or combination. | ISO-NE Schedule 22 LGIP §§3.4.2(vii) and 3.1. |
| Commercial Readiness Deposit for $100,000-study-deposit category | **2× study deposit = $200,000** | CNR-only / NR-to-CNR category using the $100,000 study deposit. | Refunded according to Schedule 22 §3.7; may be cash, irrevocable letter of credit, surety bond, or combination. | ISO-NE Schedule 22 LGIP §§3.4.1.1, 3.4.2(vii), and 3.1. |
| Site Control deposit in lieu — NR only with qualifying Regulatory Limitation | **$10,000/MW**, minimum **$500,000**, maximum **$2,000,000** | Only for NR Interconnection Service where qualifying Regulatory Limitations prevent 100% Site Control at submission. | Cash deposit held until required Site Control demonstration is provided; not available for CNR requests. | ISO-NE Schedule 22 LGIP §3.4.2(v); ISO-NE Site Control Technical Notice. |

**Form of payment/security:** Commercial Readiness Deposits may be provided by electronic cash transfer, acceptable irrevocable letter of credit, acceptable surety bond, or a combination; all other ISO-NE deposits under the LGIP must be paid in cash by electronic transfer to ISO-NE’s bank account unless otherwise stated. Source: ISO-NE Schedule 22 LGIP §3.1.

A deposit is not considered received until it is in ISO-NE’s bank account, or, for a Commercial Readiness Deposit provided as a letter of credit or surety bond, until the letter of credit or surety bond is accepted by ISO-NE. Source: ISO-NE Schedule 22 LGIP §3.1.

## 5) Minimum dollar examples

| Scenario | Required amount at submission, excluding optional/conditional items | Additional conditional amount | Source |
|---|---:|---:|---|
| New Large Generator, NR or CNR, with 100% Site Control | **$800,000** = $50,000 initial + $250,000 study + $500,000 CRD | None, if Site Control is accepted. | ISO-NE Schedule 22 LGIP §§3.4.1.1 and 3.4.2. |
| New Large Generator, NR only, qualifying Regulatory Limitation instead of Site Control | **$800,000** base deposits | Plus **$10,000/MW**, min **$500,000**, max **$2,000,000**, cash site-control-in-lieu deposit. | ISO-NE Schedule 22 LGIP §3.4.2(v); ISO-NE Site Control Technical Notice. |
| CNR-only / existing NR-to-CNR category | **$350,000** = $50,000 initial + $100,000 study + $200,000 CRD | Site Control still required for CNR. | ISO-NE Schedule 22 LGIP §§3.4.1.1 and 3.4.2. |

## 6) Deficiency review and cure

An IR is **not valid** until all items required by Schedule 22 §3.4.2 have been received by ISO-NE during the Cluster Request Window. Source: ISO-NE Schedule 22 LGIP §3.4.4.

If ISO-NE finds the IR deficient, it must notify the customer within **5 Business Days** of
